title: Aldama breviflosculosa
intro: Aldama breviflosculosa, also known as Mexican tea or yerba de los pobres, is a plant species native to Mexico and used in traditional medicine. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing pain, fever, and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Aldama breviflosculosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Aldama breviflosculosa is classified within the Asteraceae family, which includes daisies and sunflowers. It belongs to the tribe Mutisieae, a group of flowering plants characterized by their daisy-like appearance. The species is also part of the Aldama genus, which consists of plants native to the Americas. Aldama breviflosculosa is a perennial herb that grows in the wild, typically found in dry to moist areas. The classification of Aldama breviflosculosa reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants within the Asteraceae family.

benefits: Aldama breviflosculosa, being a plant species native to Mexico, has been studied for its potential health benefits.
